texts.label: text
texts.orig: For the initial distribution of software, Release Notes are optional. Revisions to a product that involve major changes to technical specifications or end-user functionality require Release Notes. Changes to software or documentation that have a minimal impact do not require Release Notes. The project manager, as the authoritative source and in consultation with the technical writer, determines if a Release Notes document is a required artifact for the project.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2 Introduction
texts.text: 2 Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) is part of the Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture (VistA) suite of application packages. CPRS enables you to enter, review, and continuously update information connected with a patient. With CPRS, you can order lab tests, medications, diets, radiology tests and procedures, record a patient’s allergies or adverse reactions to medications, request and track consults, enter progress notes, diagnoses, and treatments for each encounter, and enter discharge summaries. In addition, CPRS supports clinical decision-making and enables you to review and analyze patient data.
texts.text: The Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) is part of the Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture (VistA) suite of application packages. CPRS enables you to enter, review, and continuously update information connected with a patient. With CPRS, you can order lab tests, medications, diets, radiology tests and procedures, record a patient’s allergies or adverse reactions to medications, request and track consults, enter progress notes, diagnoses, and treatments for each encounter, and enter discharge summaries. In addition, CPRS supports clinical decision-making and enables you to review and analyze patient data.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This document describes enhancements and defect corrections included in CPRS v33SWD, consisting of a combined build, and a Graphical User Interface (GUI) executable, and a Dynamic Link Library (DLL). The combined build for CPRS v33SWD includes three patches: OR*3.0*608, TIU*1.0*318, and YS*5.01*237.
texts.text: This document describes enhancements and defect corrections included in CPRS v33SWD, consisting of a combined build, and a Graphical User Interface (GUI) executable, and a Dynamic Link Library (DLL). The combined build for CPRS v33SWD includes three patches: OR*3.0*608, TIU*1.0*318, and YS*5.01*237.

texts.orig: 5.2 Enhancements and Modifications to Existing Features
texts.text: 5.2 Enhancements and Modifications to Existing Features

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following are the enhancements and modifications for CPRS v33SWD. Many are New Service Requests (NSRs):
texts.text: The following are the enhancements and modifications for CPRS v33SWD. Many are New Service Requests (NSRs):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: NSR 20110405 – Notifications from Consults - Part 1
texts.text: NSR 20110405 – Notifications from Consults - Part 1

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Notifications from Consults aims to overhaul the approach to generating notifications for actions taken on consults and procedures. The overarching goal is to better separate clinical and administrative actions, and to generate notifications only to the users needing to take action and/or be informed about updates.
texts.text: Notifications from Consults aims to overhaul the approach to generating notifications for actions taken on consults and procedures. The overarching goal is to better separate clinical and administrative actions, and to generate notifications only to the users needing to take action and/or be informed about updates.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A small portion of this NSR is being delivered in this version of CPRS. The text displayed on the Add Comment to Consult action dialog is being updated. The aim of the text update is to better inform users as to when the ordering provider will be notified for an Add Comment to Consult action, and also prevent users from needlessly sending notifications to providers when no action is required on the provider’s part.
texts.text: A small portion of this NSR is being delivered in this version of CPRS. The text displayed on the Add Comment to Consult action dialog is being updated. The aim of the text update is to better inform users as to when the ordering provider will be notified for an Add Comment to Consult action, and also prevent users from needlessly sending notifications to providers when no action is required on the provider’s part.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The current text is generated based on the role and update authority of who is taking the action and can be one of three versions:
texts.text: The current text is generated based on the role and update authority of who is taking the action and can be one of three versions:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: “An alert will automatically be sent to the ordering provider.”
texts.text: “An alert will automatically be sent to the ordering provider.”

texts.label: text
texts.orig: “An alert will automatically be sent to notification recipients for this service.”
texts.text: “An alert will automatically be sent to notification recipients for this service.”

texts.label: text
texts.orig: “An alert will automatically be sent to the ordering provider and to notification recipients for this service.”
texts.text: “An alert will automatically be sent to the ordering provider and to notification recipients for this service.”

texts.label: text
texts.orig: With this release, the text has been updated and, only during the Add Comment to Consult action, regardless of user role/update authority, will be the following.
texts.text: With this release, the text has been updated and, only during the Add Comment to Consult action, regardless of user role/update authority, will be the following.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: “Only alert Providers when their action is required.”
texts.text: “Only alert Providers when their action is required.”

texts.label: text
texts.orig: “++ NOTE: To alert Provider use “Send additional alerts” option below, or use Significant Findings. ++”
texts.text: “++ NOTE: To alert Provider use “Send additional alerts” option below, or use Significant Findings. ++”

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: NSR 20130305: Gender/Sex (SIGI) Field in CPRS
texts.text: NSR 20130305: Gender/Sex (SIGI) Field in CPRS

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Currently, VHA CPRS displays a patient’s biological sex but does not display gender identity. These are related but distinct demographic characteristics. There are clinical and medicolegal reasons to capture both as distinct data elements. This NSR request is to change the label “Sex” to “Birth Sex” and add Self-Identified Gender Identity (SIGI) to demographic displays. There will also be context sensitive help where appropriate.
texts.text: Currently, VHA CPRS displays a patient’s biological sex but does not display gender identity. These are related but distinct demographic characteristics. There are clinical and medicolegal reasons to capture both as distinct data elements. This NSR request is to change the label “Sex” to “Birth Sex” and add Self-Identified Gender Identity (SIGI) to demographic displays. There will also be context sensitive help where appropriate.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Below is a screen capture showing the change of labels from Sex to Birth Sex and where context sensitive help was added to the patient selection screen:

texts.orig: Below is an example of the context sensitive help on patient selection screen for SIGI:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: NSR 20131005: Patient Selection Similar Patients Box
texts.text: NSR 20131005: Patient Selection Similar Patients Box

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This NSR aims to reduce the likelihood of selecting the wrong patient, a patient safety issue. To assist the user in selecting the correct patient, the patient selection screen has been modified to include additional information: Primary Care Provider, Attending Physician, Last Visit, and Last Visit Date and Time.
texts.text: This NSR aims to reduce the likelihood of selecting the wrong patient, a patient safety issue. To assist the user in selecting the correct patient, the patient selection screen has been modified to include additional information: Primary Care Provider, Attending Physician, Last Visit, and Last Visit Date and Time.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Below is an example of the additional information that displays on the patient selection screen when the user highlights a patient name.
texts.text: Below is an example of the additional information that displays on the patient selection screen when the user highlights a patient name.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In addition to the information displaying on the main screen, when the user selects a patient and there are other patients with similar names, the Similar Patient dialog displays and shows the additional information also. For the pop-up box, physician (dependent on the last visit), last location, and last visit have been added.
texts.text: In addition to the information displaying on the main screen, when the user selects a patient and there are other patients with similar names, the Similar Patient dialog displays and shows the additional information also. For the pop-up box, physician (dependent on the last visit), last location, and last visit have been added.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: NSR 20150513: Functionality of CPRS Weight Display
texts.text: NSR 20150513: Functionality of CPRS Weight Display

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS has been modified to allow sites to change how certain vitals are displayed on the coversheet, in the encounter portion of the Note display on the Notes tab, and on the Vitals tab in the encounter form. By division or for the entire system, a site may now choose to display either metric or imperial values first, for those vitals that have both.
texts.text: CPRS has been modified to allow sites to change how certain vitals are displayed on the coversheet, in the encounter portion of the Note display on the Notes tab, and on the Vitals tab in the encounter form. By division or for the entire system, a site may now choose to display either metric or imperial values first, for those vitals that have both.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In addition to the coversheet, Notes tab and the Encounter form have also been enhanced to display these values in the order determined by the new parameter. The coversheet and Encounter form will also display both values in a single column.
texts.text: In addition to the coversheet, Notes tab and the Encounter form have also been enhanced to display these values in the order determined by the new parameter. The coversheet and Encounter form will also display both values in a single column.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Vitals Pane on the Coversheet with the system set to display metric values first:
texts.text: Vitals Pane on the Coversheet with the system set to display metric values first:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Excerpt of the Notes tab, showing the encounter information with the system set to show metric values first:
texts.text: Excerpt of the Notes tab, showing the encounter information with the system set to show metric values first:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Vitals tab on the Encounter form, showing metric and imperial values for weight in a single column. The metric value shows first since the system is set to show metric first.
texts.text: Vitals tab on the Encounter form, showing metric and imperial values for weight in a single column. The metric value shows first since the system is set to show metric first.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: NSR 20160415: Progress Note Flag Actions Sort (SHRPE)
texts.text: NSR 20160415: Progress Note Flag Actions Sort (SHRPE)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Progress Note Flag Actions Sort (SHRPE) allows CPRS to display the “Originating Facility” data in the Progress Notes Properties dialog’s unlinked Patient Record Flags (PRF) flag actions section.
texts.text: Progress Note Flag Actions Sort (SHRPE) allows CPRS to display the “Originating Facility” data in the Progress Notes Properties dialog’s unlinked Patient Record Flags (PRF) flag actions section.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: NSR 20201003: Add Pronouns to Demographics bar next to new SIGI field
texts.text: NSR 20201003: Add Pronouns to Demographics bar next to new SIGI field

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The LGBTQ+ Health Program Office is requesting an enhancement in the VistA Registration and Enrollment (REE) application and Master Patient Index (MPI).  Currently, there is not a Preferred Pronoun field that reflects a person’s gender identity and expression. Requesting that a Preferred Pronoun field be added in VistA REE and MPI, so it can be used with direct patient care and exchanged with Cerner Millennium.  Preferred pronoun refers to the pronouns that reflect a person’s gender identity and expression (e.g., he/him/his, she/her/hers, they/them/theirs, etc.).
texts.text: The LGBTQ+ Health Program Office is requesting an enhancement in the VistA Registration and Enrollment (REE) application and Master Patient Index (MPI).  Currently, there is not a Preferred Pronoun field that reflects a person’s gender identity and expression. Requesting that a Preferred Pronoun field be added in VistA REE and MPI, so it can be used with direct patient care and exchanged with Cerner Millennium.  Preferred pronoun refers to the pronouns that reflect a person’s gender identity and expression (e.g., he/him/his, she/her/hers, they/them/theirs, etc.).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The benefit of this enhancement request is to provide inclusive, affirming care for transgender and gender diverse patients that will help Veterans feel welcome at VHA facilities. Non-inclusive care (e.g., misgendering Veterans) leads to patient safety risks and the risk of lawsuits.
texts.text: The benefit of this enhancement request is to provide inclusive, affirming care for transgender and gender diverse patients that will help Veterans feel welcome at VHA facilities. Non-inclusive care (e.g., misgendering Veterans) leads to patient safety risks and the risk of lawsuits.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As part of this request, there was a request to display pronouns next to the SIGI field in the patient demographics “box” in CPRS.
texts.text: As part of this request, there was a request to display pronouns next to the SIGI field in the patient demographics “box” in CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: IMPORTANT NOTE:
texts.text: IMPORTANT NOTE:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As of the release of CPRS v33SWD, no method has been deployed for entering the pronoun. The new fields have been added to the PATIENT file (#2). While not advisable, in an urgent situation that could be helped by entering a Veteran’s pronouns, if there is a department at your medical center that has FileMan access to update the PATIENT file (#2), they may update the field using FileMan.
texts.text: As of the release of CPRS v33SWD, no method has been deployed for entering the pronoun. The new fields have been added to the PATIENT file (#2). While not advisable, in an urgent situation that could be helped by entering a Veteran’s pronouns, if there is a department at your medical center that has FileMan access to update the PATIENT file (#2), they may update the field using FileMan.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Of pronouns have been entered, the following illustrates how pronoun will display.
texts.text: Of pronouns have been entered, the following illustrates how pronoun will display.

texts.orig: This example shows the pronouns She/Her and They/Them.
texts.text: This example shows the pronouns She/Her and They/Them.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: NSR 20220818: Limit Date Selection for CPRS Orders in the Extreme Future
texts.text: NSR 20220818: Limit Date Selection for CPRS Orders in the Extreme Future

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The VHA Office of Informatics and Analytics is requesting the ability to limit the Clinically Indicated Date (CID) in CPRS for future orders to no more than 390 days when ordering diagnostic exams in CPRS to prevent delaying diagnosis and treatment of patients. This release of CPRS addresses Imaging and Consult orders. Lab orders will be addressed in a future release.
texts.text: The VHA Office of Informatics and Analytics is requesting the ability to limit the Clinically Indicated Date (CID) in CPRS for future orders to no more than 390 days when ordering diagnostic exams in CPRS to prevent delaying diagnosis and treatment of patients. This release of CPRS addresses Imaging and Consult orders. Lab orders will be addressed in a future release.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: New Version of Mental Health DLL
texts.text: New Version of Mental Health DLL

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This release of CPRS GUI also includes an updated version of the Mental Health DLL, YS_MHA_A_XE10.dll, version 1.0.5.16. Due to the CPRS project moving to a new version of the software used to create CPRS (Delphi version 11.3), an updated version of the DLL was required. This is a technical enhancement only, there are no user-facing enhancements in this updated DLL.
texts.text: This release of CPRS GUI also includes an updated version of the Mental Health DLL, YS_MHA_A_XE10.dll, version 1.0.5.16. Due to the CPRS project moving to a new version of the software used to create CPRS (Delphi version 11.3), an updated version of the DLL was required. This is a technical enhancement only, there are no user-facing enhancements in this updated DLL.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: CPRS Upgrade to Delphi 11.3 Affecting Cursor Location on Right-Click and Paste
texts.text: CPRS Upgrade to Delphi 11.3 Affecting Cursor Location on Right-Click and Paste

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The CPRS GUI is created using a software product named Delphi
texts.text: The CPRS GUI is created using a software product named Delphi

texts.label: text
texts.orig: TM
texts.text: TM

texts.label: text
texts.orig: by Embarcadero Inc. This release of CPRS GUI has moved from Delphi 10.4 to Delphi 11.3. Oftentimes, users will not notice any changes when CPRS upgrades to a new version of Delphi. One change in behavior however has been noted regarding the cursor position when working in a text field.
texts.text: by Embarcadero Inc. This release of CPRS GUI has moved from Delphi 10.4 to Delphi 11.3. Oftentimes, users will not notice any changes when CPRS upgrades to a new version of Delphi. One change in behavior however has been noted regarding the cursor position when working in a text field.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In previous releases of Delphi, upon right-clicking in a text field (when pasting, for example) the cursor position would remain unchanged.
texts.text: In previous releases of Delphi, upon right-clicking in a text field (when pasting, for example) the cursor position would remain unchanged.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In Delphi 11, a more modern version of the Microsoft Windows text field is being used. Right-clicking in a text field will now move the cursor to wherever the right click occurred. This change brings Delphi-based application in line with the behavior found in modern Windows applications, including Office 365.
texts.text: In Delphi 11, a more modern version of the Microsoft Windows text field is being used. Right-clicking in a text field will now move the cursor to wherever the right click occurred. This change brings Delphi-based application in line with the behavior found in modern Windows applications, including Office 365.

texts.text: When taking the “Release Delayed Orders” action on an order in CPRS GUI, the OK and Cancel buttons begin to appear cutoff at font size 10. At font size 12, the cutoff is very noticeable and at font size 14, the buttons are no longer visible.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5.4 Patient Safety Issues (PSIs)
texts.text: 5.4 Patient Safety Issues (PSIs)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: HITPS 196 –
texts.text: HITPS 196 –

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Allergy ingredient order checks not triggering. Released in ​CPRS v32a (OR*3.0*539).​ CPRS v33SWD corrects a defect where a double-click on a causative agent lookup can bypass the warning checkbox confirming correct selection item​.
texts.text: Allergy ingredient order checks not triggering. Released in ​CPRS v32a (OR*3.0*539).​ CPRS v33SWD corrects a defect where a double-click on a causative agent lookup can bypass the warning checkbox confirming correct selection item​.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: HITPS 521 (also PSPO 540):
texts.text: HITPS 521 (also PSPO 540):

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Requesting popup box or a flag alerting staff when there are two or more patients with same last name. Corrected by NSR 20131005.
texts.text: Requesting popup box or a flag alerting staff when there are two or more patients with same last name. Corrected by NSR 20131005.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: HITPS 1596 (PSI-07-087, Remedy Ticket #189451):
texts.text: HITPS 1596 (PSI-07-087, Remedy Ticket #189451):

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Addresses the issue of the likelihood of the wrong patient being chosen when two patients have similar names. To prevent the likelihood of this occurring, each chosen patient will show the patient’s Inpatient Provider, Primary Care Provider, Last Location Name, and Last Visit Date. Corrected by NSR 20131005.
texts.text: Addresses the issue of the likelihood of the wrong patient being chosen when two patients have similar names. To prevent the likelihood of this occurring, each chosen patient will show the patient’s Inpatient Provider, Primary Care Provider, Last Location Name, and Last Visit Date. Corrected by NSR 20131005.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: HITPS 2453 (also PSPO 3657):
texts.text: HITPS 2453 (also PSPO 3657):

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patient Safety Issue with “Add Comment to Consult” dialog box. Corrected by NSR 20110405.
texts.text: Patient Safety Issue with “Add Comment to Consult” dialog box. Corrected by NSR 20110405.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: HITPS 7598:
texts.text: HITPS 7598:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Radiology Orders with Scheduled Dates Greater than 390 Days. Corrected by NSR 20220818.
texts.text: Radiology Orders with Scheduled Dates Greater than 390 Days. Corrected by NSR 20220818.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5.5 Defects
texts.text: 5.5 Defects

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC18782546 - CPRS - Display of some vitals are duplicated in the PCE display area below the progress note view.
texts.text: INC18782546 - CPRS - Display of some vitals are duplicated in the PCE display area below the progress note view.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When displaying the patient’s vitals below the note editor, sometimes the Weight information is displayed twice. This happens because the result string for function FormatVitalForNote was not being properly initialized, thus “defaulting” to the previous return value in some cases.
texts.text: When displaying the patient’s vitals below the note editor, sometimes the Weight information is displayed twice. This happens because the result string for function FormatVitalForNote was not being properly initialized, thus “defaulting” to the previous return value in some cases.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The result string for function FormatVitalForNote is now set to '' (empty string) on entering.
texts.text: The result string for function FormatVitalForNote is now set to '' (empty string) on entering.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC11869026 – Available Reminders window Scrolling Issue
texts.text: INC11869026 – Available Reminders window Scrolling Issue

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Duplicates: INC29725716, INC29684548
texts.text: Duplicates: INC29725716, INC29684548

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the Available Reminders form in CPRS, the DUE DATES and other information aren’t scrolling when the mouse wheel is used to scroll the tree of reminders.
texts.text: In the Available Reminders form in CPRS, the DUE DATES and other information aren’t scrolling when the mouse wheel is used to scroll the tree of reminders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Available Reminders form overrides the mouse wheel handling to keep both right and left sides of the reminders in sync.
texts.text: The Available Reminders form overrides the mouse wheel handling to keep both right and left sides of the reminders in sync.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC13824577 - VISN 23 has frequent reports of consult order dialogs disappearing after data is entered and before it is accepted or signed.
texts.text: INC13824577 - VISN 23 has frequent reports of consult order dialogs disappearing after data is entered and before it is accepted or signed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When the Order a Consult form is opened from an order menu, and the user selects the Lexicon menu to choose a diagnosis, sometimes the Order a Consult form is getting hidden behind the order menu.
texts.text: When the Order a Consult form is opened from an order menu, and the user selects the Lexicon menu to choose a diagnosis, sometimes the Order a Consult form is getting hidden behind the order menu.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After closing the Lexicon form, the Order a Consult form brings itself to the front of the stack of open CPRS forms.
texts.text: After closing the Lexicon form, the Order a Consult form brings itself to the front of the stack of open CPRS forms.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC12188466 - In this new version of CPRS, when I use a template, the lines that should be blank now have a space at the beginning, and the lines that should have a space at the end do not.
texts.text: INC12188466 - In this new version of CPRS, when I use a template, the lines that should be blank now have a space at the beginning, and the lines that should have a space at the end do not.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When inserting boilerplate templates, lines with formatting spaces at the end are having those spaces stripped. And what were blank lines in the template now have a space.
texts.text: When inserting boilerplate templates, lines with formatting spaces at the end are having those spaces stripped. And what were blank lines in the template now have a space.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Lines shorter than 70 characters are no longer subject to having ending whitespace removed, and blank lines are restored.
texts.text: Lines shorter than 70 characters are no longer subject to having ending whitespace removed, and blank lines are restored.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC16329726 - CPRS coversheet reminder view Education Topic Definition not working.
texts.text: INC16329726 - CPRS coversheet reminder view Education Topic Definition not working.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When working with clinical reminders in CPRS GUI, right-clicking on a reminder definition produces a context menu. This can be seen from the Notes Tab Reminders drawer, Available Reminders window shown when clicking on the Reminders clock, or the Clinical Reminders pane of the Cover Sheet.
texts.text: When working with clinical reminders in CPRS GUI, right-clicking on a reminder definition produces a context menu. This can be seen from the Notes Tab Reminders drawer, Available Reminders window shown when clicking on the Reminders clock, or the Clinical Reminders pane of the Cover Sheet.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When a reminder definition contains education topics, one of the available options on the context menu is to see the details about any of those education topics.
texts.text: When a reminder definition contains education topics, one of the available options on the context menu is to see the details about any of those education topics.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: (Reminder Options… > Education Topic Definition > Choose topic from the list).
texts.text: (Reminder Options… > Education Topic Definition > Choose topic from the list).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In a previous release of CPRS GUI, the education topic detail inquiry was inadvertently changed and tried to retrieve the reminder definition details. This would result in no data being returned “NO RECORDS TO PRINT”, or potentially incorrect data being returned if an internal entry number (IEN) match was found in the reminder definition file, for an education topic file IEN.
texts.text: In a previous release of CPRS GUI, the education topic detail inquiry was inadvertently changed and tried to retrieve the reminder definition details. This would result in no data being returned “NO RECORDS TO PRINT”, or potentially incorrect data being returned if an internal entry number (IEN) match was found in the reminder definition file, for an education topic file IEN.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The correct functionality has now been restored. When selecting Education Topic Definition from the context menu, the education topic detail will now be returned and displayed in CPRS GUI.
texts.text: The correct functionality has now been restored. When selecting Education Topic Definition from the context menu, the education topic detail will now be returned and displayed in CPRS GUI.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC28129476 (numerous child tickets) CPRS Spell Check Broken
texts.text: INC28129476 (numerous child tickets) CPRS Spell Check Broken

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In mid-2023, a Microsoft update to Office 365 introduced a defect to the Office functionality used by CPRS to invoke spell checking. Misspelled words were no longer appearing in the “Not in dictionary” section of the spell check window. A manual workaround was identified where users had to click on the Options button of the spell check window, and then close the
texts.text: In mid-2023, a Microsoft update to Office 365 introduced a defect to the Office functionality used by CPRS to invoke spell checking. Misspelled words were no longer appearing in the “Not in dictionary” section of the spell check window. A manual workaround was identified where users had to click on the Options button of the spell check window, and then close the

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Options window. This action restored visibility of any text appearing in the “Not in dictionary” section.
texts.text: Options window. This action restored visibility of any text appearing in the “Not in dictionary” section.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this version of CPRS, a change has been made that automates the manual workaround and keeps the Options window from being seen as it is opened and then closed. Effectively, users will see spell checking behave as it previously had before Microsoft introduced the defect.
texts.text: In this version of CPRS, a change has been made that automates the manual workaround and keeps the Options window from being seen as it is opened and then closed. Effectively, users will see spell checking behave as it previously had before Microsoft introduced the defect.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC29884189 - CPRS Diet error: Access Violation at address 00FB5694 in Module 'CPRSchart.exe'. Read Address 00000014
texts.text: INC29884189 - CPRS Diet error: Access Violation at address 00FB5694 in Module 'CPRSchart.exe'. Read Address 00000014

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS v32c (v32.515.2) introduced a potential bug where some order dialog configurations could cause an access violation when trying to create diet orders.
texts.text: CPRS v32c (v32.515.2) introduced a potential bug where some order dialog configurations could cause an access violation when trying to create diet orders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This access violation should no longer occur regardless of order dialog configuration.
texts.text: This access violation should no longer occur regardless of order dialog configuration.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC31027958 - Add legacy pop-up box to the list of boxes that will retain user sizing/settings when stretched to a different size.
texts.text: INC31027958 - Add legacy pop-up box to the list of boxes that will retain user sizing/settings when stretched to a different size.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Patient Lookup Messages window that displays when a patient requires a means test or when the patient has legacy data does not retain any user settings for sizing.
texts.text: The Patient Lookup Messages window that displays when a patient requires a means test or when the patient has legacy data does not retain any user settings for sizing.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This window will now retain size settings when the user has stretched the window to a different size than default.
texts.text: This window will now retain size settings when the user has stretched the window to a different size than default.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC29657166 (INC25256062) - Consult Reason for Request text still in reverse order.
texts.text: INC29657166 (INC25256062) - Consult Reason for Request text still in reverse order.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If a new consult includes a template, the content of the template is sometimes being inserted line-by-line backward into the “Reason for Request” field. This is due to the Windows richedit control sometimes returning 0 for the number lines in the “Reason for Request” field, and how TStrings.Text processes assigning a string.
texts.text: If a new consult includes a template, the content of the template is sometimes being inserted line-by-line backward into the “Reason for Request” field. This is due to the Windows richedit control sometimes returning 0 for the number lines in the “Reason for Request” field, and how TStrings.Text processes assigning a string.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS GUI has been updated to use the Windows Rich Edit control more directly, to stream the content into the control, rather than through the extra layer of Delphi’s abstraction classes.
texts.text: CPRS GUI has been updated to use the Windows Rich Edit control more directly, to stream the content into the control, rather than through the extra layer of Delphi’s abstraction classes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC20159582 ATTN: CLIN2/CPRS Developers - Reproducible TIU Template CPRS Crash.
texts.text: INC20159582 ATTN: CLIN2/CPRS Developers - Reproducible TIU Template CPRS Crash.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Certain combinations of required fields in nested templates, that are selected and deselected multiple times can generate an access violation in the CPRS GUI.
texts.text: Certain combinations of required fields in nested templates, that are selected and deselected multiple times can generate an access violation in the CPRS GUI.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When working in a template, CPRS keeps track of the template fields and their state (i.e., a checkbox is checked or unchecked). CPRS also tracks a list of the required fields in each template. In certain scenarios, as required items are selected or deselected, the required field list is not always updated and when CPRS tries to read that list, an access violation occurs.
texts.text: When working in a template, CPRS keeps track of the template fields and their state (i.e., a checkbox is checked or unchecked). CPRS also tracks a list of the required fields in each template. In certain scenarios, as required items are selected or deselected, the required field list is not always updated and when CPRS tries to read that list, an access violation occurs.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The required field tracking logic in CPRS GUI templates has been updated to correct the access violation.
texts.text: The required field tracking logic in CPRS GUI templates has been updated to correct the access violation.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5.6 Access Violation Troubleshooting Feature
texts.text: 5.6 Access Violation Troubleshooting Feature

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To help CPRS developers and support personnel address access violations that sometimes occur, the team has enhanced the feature that helps users report an access violation.
texts.text: To help CPRS developers and support personnel address access violations that sometimes occur, the team has enhanced the feature that helps users report an access violation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When an access violation occurs, CPRS gathers information and makes a log. The information in the log may help those troubleshooting to track down the source of the problem so that it can be addressed.
texts.text: When an access violation occurs, CPRS gathers information and makes a log. The information in the log may help those troubleshooting to track down the source of the problem so that it can be addressed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If an access violation error occurs, the user may see a dialog on the screen such as those shown below: the first one is an example of a GUI error, while the second represents a MUMPS error:
texts.text: If an access violation error occurs, the user may see a dialog on the screen such as those shown below: the first one is an example of a GUI error, while the second represents a MUMPS error:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Please select the “Log to email” button.
texts.text: Please select the “Log to email” button.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS will attempt to automatically email the log so that those troubleshooting can use it.
texts.text: CPRS will attempt to automatically email the log so that those troubleshooting can use it.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If CPRS cannot send the email automatically, it will prepopulate an email. Please send the email.
texts.text: If CPRS cannot send the email automatically, it will prepopulate an email. Please send the email.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Note:
texts.text: Note:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Most users would not find most of the log information useful, but it can be viewed. If the user selects the Click here to see what data the error log contains link, the Error Dialog will display with the contents of the log. This is an example of what that dialog might look like. You can select Log to email to send the log to troubleshooters.
texts.text: Most users would not find most of the log information useful, but it can be viewed. If the user selects the Click here to see what data the error log contains link, the Error Dialog will display with the contents of the log. This is an example of what that dialog might look like. You can select Log to email to send the log to troubleshooters.
